food_subgroup: Soft-cooked eggs with soy sauce and white pepper

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an egg food product concept; the Singapore-style half-boiled egg (runny, in a bowl with soy sauce) is a specific preparation without an exact FoodOn concept. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard Singapore-style half-boiled egg serving consists of 2 large eggs (approximately 130g total) broken into a small bowl while still warm from a boiling water bath, with the whites just barely set and the yolks completely runny and golden, drizzled with a few drops of dark soy sauce and dusted with white pepper.

nutrient_source.title: Half-Boiled Eggs — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code G00010236
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Foodo under food code G00010236 — Half-Boiled Eggs, Singapore style, 2 eggs.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — eggs have a GI of essentially 0 due to negligible carbohydrate content. The tiny carbohydrate content from dark soy sauce drizzle is negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 166mg per 2-egg serving (including soy sauce drizzle) — about 7% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit; eggs themselves are low in sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension may consume half-boiled eggs regularly; the soy sauce drizzle is the primary sodium source and can be omitted or minimised without affecting the nutritional value of the eggs.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Essentially zero glycaemic index; high protein and fat content with no carbohydrates from the eggs themselves; one of the most diabetes-friendly breakfast items available.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Half-boiled eggs are an excellent breakfast choice for diabetic patients, providing high-quality protein, healthy fats, and zero glycaemic load; the protein and fat content helps moderate the glycaemic impact of accompanying carbohydrates like kaya toast.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Eggs are a complete protein source with exceptional selenium (30mcg/100g), vitamin D (2.2mcg/100g), and vitamin B12; all three are clinically relevant for immune function and anti-inflammatory pathways in RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Half-boiled eggs are one of the most nutritionally valuable foods for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the selenium, vitamin D, vitamin B12, and omega-3 fatty acids in egg yolk all support immune regulation and reduce inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Singapore-style half-boiled eggs are a kopitiam (coffee shop) institution — two fresh eggs lowered into a closed container of just-boiled water for exactly 7–10 minutes, producing whites that are just barely set and opaque while the yolks remain completely liquid and rich. The eggs are broken into a small bowl and consumed immediately, seasoned with a few drops of dark soy sauce and a generous pinch of white pepper. They are always served as part of the Kaya Toast Set and are one of the simplest, most nutritionally complete foods in Singapore's hawker culture.
common_user_queries: how many calories in half-boiled eggs
how many eggs can I eat per day
is it safe to eat half-boiled eggs
half-boiled eggs vs hard-boiled eggs nutrition

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code G00010236 — Half-Boiled Eggs, Singapore style, 2 eggs.
notes: Cholesterol content is notably high (365mg/100g for eggs), providing approximately 475mg per 2-egg serving. Current dietary guidelines (2020 Dietary Guidelines) do not set an upper limit for dietary cholesterol for most healthy adults; however, patients with hypercholesterolaemia should discuss egg consumption with their clinician. Selenium content (30mcg/100g) makes eggs one of the best dietary sources of selenium in the Singapore diet.
